attributes.description: AWS RDS dashboard adapted from the [AWS LAMBDA] / [AWS EC2] WIP layouts. Pulls per-database metrics from `metrics-awscloudwatchreceiver.otel-default` (CloudWatch metric streams) and falls back to `logs-*` for recent log lines.
attributes.title: [AWS RDS] Nico WIP

embeddableConfig.description:
Monitor AWS RDS database instances across your fleet in real time.
Filter by DB instance at the top. Spot saturated CPU, idle databases, and storage outliers in the summary table — click any database to drill down, or jump to its logs and traces.
To get started, instrument your RDS instances with the AWS integration.

embeddableConfig.title: Database count over time
embeddableConfig.description: Number of RDS instances reporting metrics per 5-minute bucket, broken down by health (total / saturated / low_storage / healthy). An instance is classified by the worst signal it reported in that bucket — CPU > 80% → saturated, else FreeStorageSpace < 5 GiB → low_storage, else healthy (includes idle databases with 0 connections). The three health buckets sum to total.

attributes.title: Database count over time
attributes.description: Number of RDS instances reporting metrics per 5-minute bucket, broken down by health (total / saturated / low_storage / healthy). An instance is classified by the worst signal it reported in that bucket — CPU > 80% → saturated, else FreeStorageSpace < 5 GiB → low_storage, else healthy (includes idle databases with 0 connections). The three health buckets sum to total.

query.esql: FROM "metrics-awscloudwatchreceiver.otel-default" | EVAL DBInstanceIdentifier = attributes.DBInstanceIdentifier | WHERE LENGTH(TO_STRING(DBInstanceIdentifier)) > 0 | STATS b_cpu = MAX(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), b_storage = MIN(CASE(attributes.stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)) BY timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5 minutes), db = DBInstanceIdentifier | EVAL state = CASE(         b_cpu > 80, "saturated",         b_storage < 5368709120, "low_storage",         "healthy") | STATS total = COUNT(*),         saturated = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "saturated",         low_storage = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "low_storage",         healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy" BY timestamp | SORT timestamp ASC

FROM "metrics-awscloudwatchreceiver.otel-default" | EVAL DBInstanceIdentifier = attributes.DBInstanceIdentifier | WHERE LENGTH(TO_STRING(DBInstanceIdentifier)) > 0 | STATS b_cpu = MAX(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), b_storage = MIN(CASE(attributes.stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)) BY timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5 minutes), db = DBInstanceIdentifier | EVAL state = CASE(         b_cpu > 80, "saturated",         b_storage < 5368709120, "low_storage",         "healthy") | STATS total = COUNT(*),         saturated = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "saturated",         low_storage = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "low_storage",         healthy = COUNT(*) WHERE state == "healthy" BY timestamp | SORT timestamp ASC

embeddableConfig.title: Databases
embeddableConfig.description: Per-database metrics aggregated over the selected time range. State column is badge-colored (saturated / low-storage / idle / healthy). Units: CPU is %, Connections is count, Free mem and Free storage are GB, Read/Write IOPS are ops/s, Read/Write lat are milliseconds. Click a DB instance cell for the action menu: filter the dashboard, view logs in Discover, open Performance Insights, or open the RDS console.

query.esql: FROM "metrics-awscloudwatchreceiver.otel-default" | EVAL DBInstanceIdentifier = attributes.DBInstanceIdentifier | WHERE LENGTH(TO_STRING(DBInstanceIdentifier)) > 0 | STATS c_cpu_max = MAX(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), c_conn_max = MAX(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)), c_storage_min = MIN(CASE(attributes.stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)), c_cpu = AVG(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/CPUUtilization`, NULL)), c_conn = AVG(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/DatabaseConnections`, NULL)), c_mem = AVG(CASE(attributes.stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeableMemory`, NULL)) / 1073741824.0, c_storage = AVG(CASE(attributes.stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeStorageSpace`, NULL)) / 1073741824.0, c_read_iops = AVG(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/ReadIOPS`, NULL)), c_write_iops = AVG(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/WriteIOPS`, NULL)), c_read_lat = AVG(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/ReadLatency`, NULL)) * 1000, c_write_lat = AVG(CASE(attributes.stat == "Maximum", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/WriteLatency`, NULL)) * 1000, c_region = MAX(cloud.region) BY DBInstanceIdentifier | EVAL c_state_sort = CASE(         c_cpu_max > 80, 1,         c_storage_min < 5368709120, 2,         c_conn_max == 0 OR c_conn_max IS NULL, 3,         4),       c_state = CASE(         c_cpu_max > 80, "saturated",         c_storage_min < 5368709120, "low-storage",         c_conn_max == 0 OR c_conn_max IS NULL, "idle",         "healthy") | SORT c_state_sort ASC

FROM "metrics-awscloudwatchreceiver.otel-default" | EVAL DBInstanceIdentifier = attributes.DBInstanceIdentifier | WHERE LENGTH(TO_STRING(DBInstanceIdentifier)) > 0 AND LENGTH(TO_STRING(CASE(attributes.stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeableMemory`, NULL))) > 0 | STATS metric = AVG(CASE(attributes.stat == "Average", `metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/RDS/FreeableMemory`, NULL)) / 1073741824.0 BY timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5 minutes), DBInstanceIdentifier | SORT timestamp ASC

embeddableConfig.title: RDS logs (with fallback)
embeddableConfig.description: Recent log lines from `logs-*`. Where available, the DB instance name is extracted from `/aws/rds/instance/<name>/...` log groups; otherwise the full log group is shown. If RDS engine logs are not collected in this cluster, recent logs across `logs-*` are shown.

attributes.title: RDS logs (with fallback)
attributes.description: Recent log lines from `logs-*`. Where available, the DB instance name is extracted from `/aws/rds/instance/<name>/...` log groups; otherwise the full log group is shown. If RDS engine logs are not collected in this cluster, recent logs across `logs-*` are shown.

query.esql: FROM "logs-*" | EVAL db_instance = COALESCE(event.dataset, data_stream.dataset, "unknown") | EVAL log_stream = COALESCE(agent.name, event.dataset, data_stream.dataset, "unknown") | EVAL ts = @timestamp | EVAL msg = message | WHERE LENGTH(TO_STRING(msg)) > 0 | KEEP db_instance, log_stream, ts, msg | SORT ts DESC | LIMIT 100

FROM "logs-*" | EVAL db_instance = COALESCE(event.dataset, data_stream.dataset, "unknown") | EVAL log_stream = COALESCE(agent.name, event.dataset, data_stream.dataset, "unknown") | EVAL ts = @timestamp | EVAL msg = message | WHERE LENGTH(TO_STRING(msg)) > 0 | KEEP db_instance, log_stream, ts, msg | SORT ts DESC | LIMIT 100

embeddableConfig.title: RDS databases — hexagon map (click an hex for actions)
embeddableConfig.description: One hexagon per RDS database, ordered worst-first (connection-saturated or low-storage databases appear top-left). Toggle 'Color by' between Status (healthy / connection-saturated / storage-low / idle), CPU (utilization %) or Free Storage (GB - lower values colour red because less free space is worse). Gauge metrics support Min / Max / Avg / Last over the dashboard time range. Click any hexagon for a drilldown menu: filter the dashboard, or jump into logs, metrics or APM traces for that database.
